name: stabilizing-rl-diffusion
description: Apply Stabilizing RL for Diffusion Language Models to stabilizing problems. Technique: Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is highly effective for post-training ...
paperTitle: Stabilizing RL for Diffusion Language Models
content:
## Overview

This skill implements the technique from "Stabilizing RL for Diffusion Language Models".

Key contribution: Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is highly effective for post-training autoregressive (AR) language models, yet its direct application to diffusion large language models (dLLMs) often triggers reward collapse. We identify two sources of incompatibility.

## When to use

- Apply when solving stabilizing problems
- When baseline approaches are insufficient
- Working with structured problem spaces

## When NOT to use

- If problem constraints limit complexity
- When simpler methods achieve target performance
- Time-critical scenarios with strict latency needs

## Core Technique

The paper proposes an approach through:

1. Problem formulation from the paper
2. Algorithm implementation as described
3. Integration with existing systems
4. Evaluation against baselines

## Implementation

1. Understand problem requirements
2. Implement core algorithm components
3. Configure evaluation metrics from paper
4. Compare against baseline methods
5. Tune hyperparameters for your domain

## References

Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.06743
